_README: Master tile-to-product map for ALL editorial stories in The Current. Each tile has a unique ID, its story, position number, whether it's a product (clickable) or not, and if product — the exact genomeKey to use. This is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH. The React code should import this file and look up tile data by ID. Last updated: Feb 25, 2026 — includes all Sharon screenshot corrections.

morocco._correction_note: REBUILT Feb 26, 2026 from Sharon's definitive layout. Key changes: pos 1 is YSL LouLou Bikini (was Gaia Dress), pos 2 is Gaia Dress (was place), pos 3 name corrected to Souk Coat/Desert Pant, pos 7 is FDV Column Dress (was YSL Swimwear), pos 9 changed from Cecily Dress to place (Column Dress moved to pos 7). 6 style products total: positions 1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 11.

morocco-1.caption: Amanjena, Marrakech. Desert-toned walls, long shadows, stillness. A black bathing suit against all that sand and clay feels moody and intentional.
morocco-1.brandLine: LouLou Bikini by Yves Saint Laurent — Coming Soon
morocco-1.correction: Feb 26 correction — was Phoebe Philo Gaia Dress, should be YSL LouLou Bikini (bathing suit is the FIRST image)

morocco-2.caption: Crisp black cotton against terracotta. The contrast is the point.
morocco-2.brandLine: Gaia Dress by Phoebe Philo — Coming Soon
morocco-2.description: Crisp lightweight cotton long dress with side slit panels and low neckline
morocco-2.correction: Feb 26 correction — was a place, is actually a product (Gaia Dress)

morocco-3.caption: Blush pink against sun-warmed clay. Soft, but never sweet. The color almost disappears into the earth — and that's the beauty of it.
morocco-3.brandLine: Souk Coat / Desert Pant by Alaïa — Coming Soon
morocco-3.description: Cotton viscose gabardine half lightweight coat with cotton viscose gabardine full pants with layered leg and layered waist
morocco-3.note: genomeKey still uses DRAPEDTOP — closest match in genome. Product name corrected Feb 26.
morocco-3.correction: Feb 26 correction — product name was 'Look', should be 'Souk Coat / Desert Pant'

morocco-4.caption: El Fenn. Layered ruby red, saffron, emerald, tile, velvet. It's saturated and fearless, but somehow still composed.

morocco-5.caption: Black against warm limestone — a long, clean silhouette moving through arches. The dress absorbs the light instead of competing with it.
morocco-5.brandLine: Este Dress by FIL DE VIE — Coming Soon

morocco-6.caption: Orange blossom in bloom. That faint citrus-sweet scent in the air — orange blossom drifting through the heat. Color everywhere, but the fragrance is what lingers.

morocco-7.caption: What travels well here. Choose pieces that feel intentional, not precious. A simple black column dress, a sandal you can walk up the stairs in.
morocco-7.brandLine: Column Dress by FIL DE VIE — Coming Soon
morocco-7.correction: Feb 26 correction — was YSL Swimwear, should be FDV Column Dress

morocco-8.caption: El Fenn Hotel. Graphic tile, citrus trees overhead. Pattern here is rhythmic.

morocco-9.caption: What travels well here. Choose pieces that feel intentional, not precious.
morocco-9.correction: Feb 26 correction — was Cecily Dress (style), now place. Column Dress product moved to position 7.

morocco-10.title: SHADES OF NEUTRAL
morocco-10.caption: Terracotta walls, late afternoon sunlight on a single plane of red clay. You start noticing how many shades of 'neutral' exist here.

morocco-11.caption: El Fenn. A flash of red against layered tile. Pattern on pattern — and then that one bold interruption.
morocco-11.brandLine: Long Caftan Dress by FIL DE VIE — Coming Soon

morocco-12.title: EVENINGS DONE PROPERLY
morocco-12.caption: El Fenn. Gold light pooling over stone and olive branches. At night the color softens, but it never disappears.

hydra-1.caption: There are no cars waiting. No rush to check in. You step off the ferry and the island lowers the volume for you.

hydra-2.caption: Hydra favors clean lines and bare ankles. Nothing that competes with the light. You dress once and repeat without thinking.
hydra-2.brandLine: Jumpsuit by FIL DE VIE. Coming soon.
hydra-2.note: Tile says FDV but no FDV jumpsuit in genome. Mapped to YSL Jumpsuit.

hydra-3.caption: Rooftops baked in sun. Boats tucked into narrow slips. Salt air everywhere.

hydra-4.caption: Wind off the harbor, oversized tailoring thrown over bare skin. Hydra makes even structure feel relaxed.
hydra-4.brandLine: Shirt by Jil Sander. Coming Soon.
hydra-4.correction: Sharon correction — was Trench Coat, should be Button Down Shirt (blue stripe)

hydra-5.caption: Lunch is simple. Bread, tomatoes, cold wine, the harbor in front of you.

hydra-6.caption: White against deeper blue. Fabric moving in the breeze. It's less about the outfit and more about how it catches air.
hydra-6.brandLine: Look by Jil Sander. Coming Soon.
hydra-6.correction: Sharon correction — was Silk Fluid Set (black), should be Trench Coat (white, $1230)

hydra-7.caption: Stone stacked into cliff. No symmetry, just instinct and time. Hydra feels built by hand.

hydra-8.caption: Black swimwear against chalk-white stone. Clean and unfussy.
hydra-8.correction: Sharon screenshot 2:02 — should be Effigie Suit, not generic Eres

hydra-9.caption: Water so clear it feels architectural. You see every movement, every ripple.

hydra-10.caption: Bring objects that disappear when worn. A sandal you forget you're wearing. A shirt that flies in the wind.
hydra-10.brandLine: Blouse by Phoebe Philo. Coming soon.
hydra-10.correction: Sharon screenshot 1:58 — should be PP Button Down Shirt, was showing old format

hydra-11.caption: Harbor below, stone house above, sea wrapping everything.

hydra-12.caption: Salt, sun, then immersion. Nothing feels more right here than disappearing into water.
hydra-12.correction: Sharon correction — genomeKey was LOOK:ERES:ASIA:SWIMSUIT:BLACK.jpg (maps to 'Asymetric One Piece'), corrected to look:eres:swimsuit.jpg which has name 'Asia Swimsuit'

hydra-13.caption: Morning is for water. Midday belongs to shade, conversation and a glass of cold wine. Evening arrives without ceremony.
hydra-13.brandLine: Look by Dries Van Noten. Coming soon.
hydra-13.correction: Sharon screenshot 1:59 — should be DVN Layered Silk Dress

spain-1.caption: Black against stone. Skin warmed by late afternoon. Spain isn't about urgency — it's about letting the day stretch.

spain-2.caption: Bare shoulders. A single cuff. Clean lines that don't compete with light. Spain rewards restraint — even in heat.
spain-2.brandLine: Cuff by Phoebe Philo. Coming Soon.
spain-2.correction: Sharon screenshot 2:02 — should be PP Silver Cuff, not generic PP

spain-3.caption: Vertical cliff, striped lighthouse, sea moving slowly below. The landscape feels elemental, almost graphic.

spain-4.caption: Black swim against pale water and tree shade. The silhouette stays sharp, even when everything else is soft.
spain-4.brandLine: Swimwear by FIL DE VIE. Coming soon.

spain-5.caption: Open water. No soundtrack but wind and distant boats. Spain feels expansive without trying.
spain-5.brandLine: Look by Loro Piana. Coming soon.
spain-5.correction: Sharon screenshot 2:03 — was listed as travel/place, should be STYLE so modal opens. Loro Piana Slip Dress.

spain-6.caption: Minimal swim, clean cut, nothing ornamental. The body becomes part of the landscape.
spain-6.correction: Sharon screenshot 2:04 — was showing Gaia Dress, should be PP Polo Sweater

spain-7.caption: A path carved through stone toward the sea. You walk slower without deciding to.
spain-7.correction: Sharon screenshot 2:05 — REMOVE 'Dress by FIL DE VIE. Coming soon.' from caption. Keep as place/not clickable.

spain-8.caption: Salt hair, narrow deck, strong sun. Pieces that hold their shape against wind matter here.

spain-10.caption: Dark lenses against white sky. The light is unforgiving here — the accessories shouldn't be.
spain-10.correction: Sharon screenshot 2:06 — should be PP Bombe Sunglasses

spain-11.caption: A cove tucked into rock. Shade feels earned. Water feels earned.

spain-12.caption: Gold at the waterline. Sun, salt, skin. That's enough.
spain-12.correction: Sharon screenshot 2:07 — should be RayBan Puffed Wayfarer

spain-13.caption: Late afternoon. Linen, sunglasses, a glass in hand. Spain doesn't perform — it lingers.
spain-13.correction: Sharon screenshot 2:08 — NOT clickable, travel/destination shot. No product.

retreat-1.caption: Blending into red-rock country, this is the kind of place that doesn't compete with the landscape — it disappears into it.

retreat-2.caption: Sandstone and sky. The quiet hits first.

retreat-3.caption: Early yoga. Morning swim. Breakfast in the sun. A rhythm that feels easy to keep.

retreat-4.caption: A pool carved straight into the rock. Float. Look up. Let the sky take over.

retreat-5.caption: A room that opens onto rock and sky. Bare walls. Nothing competing with the view.
retreat-5.brandLine: Look by The Row. Coming soon.

retreat-6.caption: Clean lines. Technical fabric. Modern pieces that make sense against sun-warmed land.
retreat-6.brandLine: Look by Fear Of God. Coming soon.
retreat-6.correction: Sharon screenshot 2:10 — should be Fear of God Leather Robe Coat

retreat-7.caption: Late afternoon, stretched out in the sun. The desert has a way of softening you.
retreat-7.brandLine: Cashmere set by Phoebe Philo. Coming soon.
retreat-7.correction: Sharon screenshot 2:10 — should be PP Cashmere Sweater and Track Pant in Espresso

retreat-8.caption: Warm stone. Soft light. The kind of quiet you don't rush.
retreat-8.brandLine: Look by Aimé Leon Dore. Coming soon.

retreat-9.caption: Red rock and still water. Everything blends. Nothing shouts.

retreat-10.correction: Sharon correction — was Trench Coat (white), should be Silk Fluid Set (black)
retreat-10.caption: A long dark line against open desert. Simple. Strong. Moving through.
retreat-10.brandLine: Look by Jil Sander. Coming soon.

retreat-11.caption: Midday settles in. Shade becomes the luxury.
retreat-11.correction: Sharon screenshot 2:11 — should be Effigie Swimsuit (NOT Asia)

retreat-12.caption: Inside, cool stone. Outside, endless heat. That edge between the two is the whole mood.
retreat-12.brandLine: Look by Fear Of God. Coming soon.
retreat-12.note: Same product as retreat-6 — appears twice in story

ny-1.caption: Golden hour in Midtown never gets old. It lasts maybe ten minutes. That's the magic.

ny-2.caption: New York is made for tailoring. A long coat, sharp trousers, nothing complicated. It always works here.

ny-3.caption: Washington Square Park on a warm afternoon. Musicians, chess players, flowers, and that arch framing everything like a movie set.

ny-4.caption: The Swan Bar, Lower East Side. One of those rooms where the lighting is perfect and no one is in a rush.
ny-4.note: Venue — NOT clickable for product. Book button only.

ny-5.caption: Le CouCou. Soho. White tablecloths, chandeliers, a little bit of drama.

ny-6.caption: Downtown at night is a different city. Neon, bikes, music leaking out of somewhere you want to go.

ny-7.genomeKey: fil de vie look grecian dress.jpg
ny-7.caption: Long black fur coat under concrete columns. The city loves a strong silhouette.
ny-7.brandLine: Isabelle Coat by FIL DE VIE. Coming soon.
ny-7.correction: Sharon correction — was Mira Coat, should be Isabelle Coat. Genome key is 'fil de vie look grecian dress.jpg' (misnamed in genome but name field is correct)

ny-8.caption: A sharp black boot that can handle pavement, gallery floors, and a late-night walk home.
ny-8.correction: Sharon screenshot 2:15 — should be PP Boot

ny-9.caption: Waiting for the train, headphones in, coat moving in the draft. It's cinematic even when it's ordinary.
ny-9.brandLine: Dress & Stole by FIL DE VIE. Coming soon.

ny-10.caption: The Plaza lit up at night. Touristy? Maybe. Still iconic? Always.

ny-11.caption: The Tomb of Perneb at The Met. A museum afternoon is mandatory. Clean lines, good art, and an outfit that belongs there.
ny-11.brandLine: Look by FIL DE VIE. Coming soon.
ny-11.correction: Sharon screenshot 2:15 — was travel/not clicking. MUST be pinType style. Hera Dress.

ny-12.caption: The Guggenheim never disappoints. Walk the spiral slowly. Look up.

ny-13.caption: A bag that stays close. Sunglasses for the morning after. One piece of jewelry that works everywhere. New York asks for objects that keep up — not slow you down.
ny-13.correction: Sharon screenshot 2:16 — was not opening. Must be pinType style and clickable.

ny-14.caption: Carnegie Hill stoops in late afternoon. It feels like you've stepped into someone's movie.

ny-15.caption: A red notebook in your bag. New York gives you ideas whether you ask for them or not.
ny-15.correction: Sharon screenshot 2:16 — was listed as Cartier Diary. Corrected to Smythson Chelsea Notebook.

ny-16.caption: Good sunglasses are non-negotiable here. Even in winter.
ny-16.correction: Sharon screenshot 2:17 — modal was showing incorrect info.

ny-17.caption: Evening walk, collar up, traffic behind you. This is the version of you the city likes best.
ny-17.brandLine: Mira Coat by FIL DE VIE. Coming soon.
ny-17.correction: Sharon correction — was PP Robe Coat (showing as Gaia Dress), should be FDV Mira Coat

ny-18.caption: Central Park on a Sunday morning is for walking. No destination. No performance. Coffee in hand. Coat open. The city exhales with you.

carousels._README: SHOP THE STORY carousel items for each story. Only includes products (isProduct: true). Each entry references the tile ID + genomeKey. Claude Code should build each carousel from this list.

morocco.label: FIL DE VIE — Long Caftan Dress

carousels._morocco_note: REBUILT Feb 26. All 6 carousel items now use REAL editorial positions (no synthetic IDs). YSL LouLou Bikini and Column Dress need to be ADDED to fdv_brand_genome.json.

hydra.label: Phoebe Philo — Linen Relaxed Button Down Shirt
Dries Van Noten — Layered Silk Dress

retreat.label: Fear Of God — Leather Robe Coat
Phoebe Philo — Cashmere Sweater & Track Pant
Aimé Leon Dore — Quarter Zip Set

newyork.genomeKey: fil de vie look grecian dress.jpg
newyork.label: FIL DE VIE — Isabelle Coat
FIL DE VIE — Dara Dress & Fur Stole

closingLines.hydra: Save what the island gave you.
closingLines.retreat: Save what brought you back to yourself.

morocco.quoteCard: In Morocco, beauty is not decoration — it's architecture.

hydra.quoteCard: Salt air first. Everything else after.

spain.quoteCard: In Mallorca, afternoon is a state of mind.

newyork.quoteCard: You don't visit New York. You return to it.
